Life on a tropical island is a dream for most people. For the fortunate who can afford it, it is a dream come true on Sunset Key. Sunset Key is genuinely unique, one of the only islands in the Caribbean that offers such tropical splendor. Such magnificent architecture and design. Sunset Key, you're on a private island. The Florida Keys are known for our beautiful marine environment. We're home to the third largest barrier reef. We have visitors from around the world that come to visit to see our beautiful marine environments. While you're here, we're encouraging you to wear mineral-based sunscreens to protect your skin. Using mineral-based sunscreens is one practical choice that you can make while you're visiting the Florida Keys to help protect our marine ecosystems. We know that chemical sunscreens negatively affect our coral reefs, and our coral reefs, especially here in the Florida Keys, are struggling. Some reefs have lost 98% of coral cover. Coral reefs are extremely important to our economy in the Florida Keys, providing two-thirds of jobs, and they also keep us safe during storms. So it's really important that we protect the reefs in whatever way that we can, including by paying attention to what type of sunscreen we're putting on our skin. We want to be looking for the mineral-based sunscreens that have zinc oxide and titanium dioxide in them, and we want to be staying away from chemical sunscreens that contain ingredients like octanoxate, oxybenzone, and homocylate. The Florida Keys have not actively banned chemical sunscreens, but when we know better, we can do better. Staying away from chemical sunscreens and choosing mineral-based sunscreens won't solve every single environmental problem, but it is one practical choice that you can make while you're visiting here in the Florida Keys to help protect our ecosystems. Reef-for-Leaf has a program called Responsible Sunscreens that connect conscientious consumers with businesses that are selling these mineral-based sunscreens.
